headline: Starz Revives Mozart Drama with Electrifying Ensemble Cast
lede: Starz drops a provocative historical miniseries reimagining the cutthroat world of classical music's most notorious rivalry. Paul Bettany and Will Sharpe headline a fresh take on the legendary Salieri-Mozart conflict.
body:
Starz is set to electrify prestige television with its upcoming limited series diving deep into the tempestuous relationship between Antonio Salieri and Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart. The network's adaptation, helmed by rising director Joe Barton, promises a fresh examination of Peter Shaffer's seminal 1979 stage play.

The production assembles a powerhouse ensemble, with Paul Bettany and Will Sharpe taking center stage as the competing composers. Bettany, known for nuanced character work, seems primed to explore Salieri's complex psychological landscape of artistic jealousy and professional resentment.

Barton, whose previous work on 'Black Doves' demonstrated a keen eye for interpersonal dynamics, appears perfectly positioned to unpack the psychological warfare between two musical titans. The series will likely excavate the human drama behind the historical legends, moving beyond traditional biographical storytelling.

Starz continues its strategic pivot toward prestige historical narratives, following successful limited series like 'The Serpent Queen' and 'Becoming Elizabeth'. The Mozart drama represents another calculated bet on high-production-value storytelling that blends academic insight with dramatic tension.
key_facts: Based on Peter Shaffer's acclaimed 1979 play
Directed by Joe Barton of 'Black Doves'
Starring Paul Bettany and Will Sharpe
